insight: FastMatcher supports `#tag` syntax (e.g. `match #swords`, `match #enchantable/armor`) to match items by Minecraft tags via Bukkit's Tag API. Tags are resolved at compile time into a HashSet of material names for O(1) runtime lookup. The items registry is checked first, then blocks. Tags can be combined with regular patterns using `|` (e.g. `#swords|TRIDENT`).

FastMatcher.compile() is called by multiple callers with different case transformations (FilterItem uses toUpperCase(), FilterCommand uses toLowerCase(), Rule uses raw). Any new pattern syntax added to FastMatcher must be case-resilient since the caller may transform the input before compilation. For Minecraft tag matching (#tag), tag names must be lowercased before passing to NamespacedKey.minecraft() which rejects uppercase characters.
